header.tagline: Local vs Frontier LLM Decision Tool

explainer.title: Choose the right LLM approach
explainer.subtitle: Answer a few questions, get a recommendation in 2 minutes
explainer.localDescription: run on your hardware — no data leaves your environment, no per-token costs, but capability is limited by model size and your compute.
explainer.frontierDescription: like Claude and GPT-4 offer state-of-the-art reasoning, but data is processed externally and costs scale with usage.
explainer.rightChoiceDescription: what you're building, who sees the data, what happens if output is wrong, and what your org allows.
explainer.duration: Takes about 2 minutes. No account required.

taskSelector.title: What are you trying to do?
taskSelector.subtitle: Pick the task type that best matches your use case.
taskSelector.searchPlaceholder: Type to filter tasks... e.g., code, summarise, translate
taskSelector.noMatchesHint: Try a different keyword, or browse all tasks above.

summarisation.name: Summarisation
summarisation.description: Condense articles, reports, or documents into key points
summarisation.example: e.g., TL;DR of a research paper, meeting recap

classification.name: Classification / Tagging
classification.description: Sort content into predefined categories or labels
classification.example: e.g., ticket routing, content moderation, lead scoring

copy-editing.name: Copy Editing / Rewriting
copy-editing.description: Improve clarity, tone, grammar, or style
copy-editing.example: e.g., make this more formal, fix grammar, simplify

translation.name: Translation
translation.example: e.g., English to Spanish, localise UI strings

template-filling.name: Template / Form Filling
template-filling.description: Populate structured templates with extracted data
template-filling.example: e.g., fill invoice fields, extract entities to JSON

sentiment-analysis.name: Sentiment Analysis
sentiment-analysis.description: Detect emotional tone, opinion, or intent
sentiment-analysis.example: e.g., is this review positive?, customer mood detection

code-explanation.name: Code Explanation
code-explanation.description: Explain what code does in plain language
code-explanation.example: e.g., what does this function do?, add inline comments

qa-provided-text.name: Q&A on Provided Text
qa-provided-text.description: Answer questions using context you supply
qa-provided-text.example: e.g., ask questions about a PDF, search docs

first-draft.name: First-Draft Generation
first-draft.description: Create initial drafts that humans will refine
first-draft.example: e.g., blog post outline, email draft, proposal skeleton

test-generation.name: Test Case Generation
test-generation.description: Generate unit tests, edge cases, or test data
test-generation.example: e.g., write Jest tests for this function

meeting-notes.name: Meeting Notes to Actions
meeting-notes.description: Extract action items, decisions, and owners from notes
meeting-notes.example: e.g., parse transcript into task list with assignees

code-review.name: Code Review
code-review.description: Review code for bugs, security issues, and style
code-review.example: e.g., review this PR, find potential issues

risk-assessment.name: Risk Assessment
risk-assessment.description: Identify, evaluate, and prioritise potential risks
risk-assessment.example: e.g., security audit, compliance gap analysis

research-synthesis.name: Research Synthesis
research-synthesis.description: Combine multiple sources into coherent analysis
research-synthesis.example: e.g., literature review, competitive analysis, due diligence

autonomous-coding.name: Autonomous Coding Agent
autonomous-coding.description: Multi-step code generation with file edits and tool use
autonomous-coding.example: e.g., build this feature end-to-end, refactor module

complex-debugging.name: Complex Debugging
complex-debugging.description: Diagnose and fix non-obvious or multi-system bugs
complex-debugging.example: e.g., race condition, memory leak, integration failure

long-form-document.name: Long-Form Document
long-form-document.description: Create lengthy documents requiring multiple revisions
long-form-document.example: e.g., technical spec, whitepaper, documentation site

multi-criteria-decision.name: Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis
multi-criteria-decision.description: Evaluate options across multiple weighted factors
multi-criteria-decision.example: e.g., vendor selection, architecture trade-off analysis

chatbot-conversational.name: Chatbot / Conversational Agent
chatbot-conversational.description: Multi-turn dialogue with users, stateful conversations
chatbot-conversational.example: e.g., customer support bot, FAQ assistant, onboarding guide

data-extraction.name: Data Extraction / Parsing
data-extraction.description: Pull structured data from unstructured documents
data-extraction.example: e.g., extract fields from invoices, parse resumes, contract analysis

creative-marketing.name: Creative / Marketing Copy
creative-marketing.example: e.g., ad copy, product descriptions, social posts, taglines

outputStakes.title: What happens if the output is wrong?

hardware.title: What hardware do you have available?

toolingComfort.title: How comfortable are you with local tooling?

structuredOutput.title: Do you need structured output (JSON, etc.)?

verdict.modelsUnavailableHint: The verdict above is still valid based on your inputs.
verdict.modelsDescription: Based on your hardware and preferences. Each card shows the model, provider, and managed hosting options if available.
verdict.needMoreCapabilityDescription: These models require more resources than your current hardware, but offer stronger performance. Consider cloud deployment or hardware upgrade.

fixHierarchy.title: If local falls short, try these fixes (ranked by effort):

dpa.title: Data Processing Agreement
dpa.detail: Ensure your API provider has a DPA in place. Review their data retention and training policies.

complianceReview.title: Compliance Review Required
complianceReview.detail: Confidential data + external API requires legal/compliance sign-off. Document the business justification.

dataInHouse.title: Data Stays In-House
dataInHouse.detail: No external data transfer. Document your local deployment for audit purposes.

humanInLoop.title: Human-in-the-Loop Required
humanInLoop.detail: High-stakes output should have mandatory human review before action. Define who approves and document the process.

auditTrail.title: Audit Trail
auditTrail.detail: Log inputs, outputs, and reviewer decisions. Retain for your compliance period.

budgetControls.title: Budget Controls
budgetControls.detail: Set up spend alerts and rate limits. High volume + API = costs can spike unexpectedly.

vendorDependency.title: Vendor Dependency
vendorDependency.detail: Document fallback options. API pricing, terms, and model availability can change.

integrationGovernance.title: Integration Governance
integrationGovernance.detail: Document the data flow between local and external components. Ensure each hop is compliant.

aiDisclosure.title: User-Facing AI Disclosure
aiDisclosure.detail: Users should know they're interacting with AI. Check regional requirements (EU AI Act, etc.).

guardrails.title: Guardrails & Content Filtering
guardrails.detail: Implement input/output filtering to prevent harmful content, prompt injection, and off-topic responses.

extractionValidation.title: Extraction Accuracy Validation
extractionValidation.detail: Validate extracted data against source documents. Errors in structured data can propagate downstream.

lowOverhead.title: Low Governance Overhead
lowOverhead.detail: Your combination of low sensitivity and low stakes means minimal compliance requirements.

steps.chooseModel: Choose a model from recommendations above
steps.installOllama: Install Ollama or llama.cpp on your target machine
steps.downloadLMStudio: Download LM Studio or GPT4All for a visual interface
steps.setupManaged: Set up managed inference via Together AI or Replicate
steps.runPilot: Run a pilot with representative test cases
steps.documentDeployment: Document the deployment for your team
steps.selectProvider: Select an API provider from recommendations
steps.reviewTerms: Review provider's terms of service and DPA
steps.getApproval: Get compliance/legal approval for data processing
steps.setupApiKeys: Set up API keys with appropriate access controls
steps.implementRateLimiting: Implement rate limiting and cost monitoring
steps.designArchitecture: Design the architecture: which components are local vs external
steps.documentDataFlow: Document data flow and where sensitive data is processed
steps.setupLocalFirst: Set up local model first, then add orchestration layer
steps.testPipeline: Test the full pipeline with representative data
steps.tryLocalFirst: Try local first — lower commitment, easier to switch later
steps.upgradeIfNeeded: If local doesn't meet quality bar, upgrade to frontier API
steps.documentRationale: Document your decision rationale for future reference
steps.defineHumanReview: Define human review workflow before production use
steps.implementStateManagement: Implement conversation state management and context window handling
steps.addContentFiltering: Add content filtering and guardrails before user-facing deployment
steps.createValidationRules: Create validation rules for extracted fields
steps.buildSampleSet: Build a sample set to measure extraction accuracy before scaling
steps.defineBrandVoice: Define brand voice guidelines and review criteria

tooltips.local: Run models on your own hardware. Free after setup, fully private, but limited by your compute.
tooltips.frontier: Use cloud APIs like Claude or GPT-4. Best capability, but costs per token and sends data externally.
tooltips.hybrid: Combine local models with external tools like RAG, agents, or fine-tuning for enhanced capability.
tooltips.either: Both local and frontier approaches would work well for this use case. Choose based on preference.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.ollamaDesc: Run LLMs locally via command line. Simple setup, great for developers.
gettingStarted.lmStudioDesc: User-friendly desktop app for running local models. No coding required.
gettingStarted.gpt4allDesc: Free, privacy-focused desktop app. Works offline, easy to use.
gettingStarted.openaiDesc: Industry-leading API. Pay per token, best for production apps.
gettingStarted.claudeDesc: Anthropic's powerful API. Great for complex reasoning tasks.
gettingStarted.langchainDesc: Framework for building apps that combine local and cloud AI.

offline.banner: You're offline. Some features may be unavailable.
